answer:
Thomas brought up the rising prominence of Christianity among blacks to counter Froude's accusations of laziness, cannibalism, and devil-worship. By highlighting their adoption of Christianity, Thomas refuted these negative stereotypes and demonstrated the moral and social progress of black communities. This argument supported his broader defense of black capacity for self-governance and intellectual achievement.

\boxed{\text{Thomas brought up the rising of Christianity among blacks to counter Froude's accusations of laziness, cannibalism, and devil-worship, demonstrating their moral progress and refuting negative stereotypes.}}
